primary_evidence: Net Sharpe 0.57 at 5 bps with -15.3% max drawdown (SPY drew down -55.2%).
weaknesses: Performance is uneven across market regimes.
best_use_case: Use as a long-only de-risking trend overlay that controls drawdowns, not as a standalone return-maximizing strategy.
final_research_note: Time-Series Momentum holds up: net Sharpe 0.57, -15.3% max drawdown, and out-of-sample Sharpe 0.48. It survives realistic costs and parameter variation.

primary_evidence: Net Sharpe 0.62 at 5 bps with -18.4% max drawdown (SPY drew down -55.2%).
weaknesses: Performance is uneven across market regimes.
best_use_case: Use as a relative-strength allocation sleeve within a diversified book, not as proof of standalone alpha.
final_research_note: Cross-Sectional Momentum holds up: net Sharpe 0.62, -18.4% max drawdown, and out-of-sample Sharpe 0.73. It survives realistic costs and parameter variation.

primary_evidence: Net Sharpe 0.45 at 5 bps with -29.1% max drawdown (SPY drew down -55.2%).
weaknesses: Performance is uneven across market regimes.
best_use_case: Not recommended as a standalone allocation on this evidence; at most a low-cost tactical input, never a return-maximizing strategy.
final_research_note: Short-Term Reversal is rejected on this evidence: it does not clear simple benchmarks; net Sharpe 0.45, out-of-sample Sharpe 0.33.

primary_evidence: Net Sharpe 0.58 at 5 bps with -11.8% max drawdown (SPY drew down -55.2%).
weaknesses: Performance is uneven across market regimes.
best_use_case: Use as a volatility-control overlay when drawdown control matters more than raw return, not as a return maximizer.
final_research_note: Volatility-Scaled Momentum is conditional: it adds value mainly through risk control (net Sharpe 0.58, -11.8% max drawdown) rather than raw return, and depends on costs or regime.

primary_evidence: Net Sharpe 0.59 at 5 bps with -21.8% max drawdown (SPY drew down -55.2%).
weaknesses: Performance is uneven across market regimes.
best_use_case: Use as a diversified, lower-variance core blend, not as a single-signal bet or proof of alpha.
final_research_note: Equal-Weight Signal Ensemble holds up: net Sharpe 0.59, -21.8% max drawdown, and out-of-sample Sharpe 0.59. It survives realistic costs and parameter variation.
